components.text:
When I awoke, the sun was nearly gone. Only a few fading rays reached through the cave entrance.

My body ached, yet curiosity overcame fear.

As I approached the entrance, I discovered the strange veil was soft to the touch, like water stretched into a thin membrane. With slight pressure my hand passed through it effortlessly. I could leave and enter as I pleased, though the beasts outside could not.

Realizing night would soon fall, I gathered firewood and returned to the cave. After lighting a small fire with flint, I ate the remaining mushrooms and finally began observing my surroundings.

The cave walls were unnaturally smooth.

Upon them were symbols unlike any language I had ever seen — elegant strokes etched with impossible precision. Though ancient beyond measure, they carried a strange presence, as though the words themselves remained alive.

I fashioned a crude torch and ventured deeper.

The cave widened into what appeared to be an ancient study. A cushion rested at its center, so old it nearly crumbled into dust when touched.

Shelves and tables surrounded the room, buried beneath scattered papers crafted from materials far sturdier than common parchment.

Most writings had faded with time.

One object, however, remained untouched.

A book.

Perfect.

Not a single scratch marked its cover. It seemed untouched by age itself.

The moment I saw it, I felt drawn toward it.

When I lifted it, the weight startled me. It was heavier than any ordinary book should have been. Yet when I opened it, I discovered something even stranger.

I could read it.

The diary belonged to a man who referred to himself as a cultivator.

Within its pages were descriptions of powers beyond imagination — men who flew across the heavens, shattered mountains with a gesture, commanded storms, refined fire, and lived for centuries.

Had I not witnessed the hidden cave myself, I would have dismissed it all as madness.

But the impossible had already saved my life.

The cultivator explained that the hidden entrance was formed through an Illusion Array — one of many formations capable of deceiving the senses and manipulating the world itself.

Further within the study I discovered texts explaining the strange language carved upon the walls. It was the language of cultivators, used for techniques, arrays, talismans, and ancient records.

From that day onward, my life changed forever.

I returned to the village only long enough to gather supplies — ink, brushes, paper, oil, preserved food. Then I spent every possible moment inside the hidden cave studying.

Whenever I attempted to copy the wall inscriptions, however, something peculiar occurred.

My mind would enter a trance.

Hours vanished like moments.

When awareness returned, the paper contained only meaningless scribbles.

Yet each failure sharpened my mind slightly. My thoughts became clearer. My memory stronger. Slowly, over many attempts, I began understanding fragments of the writings.

They were cultivation techniques.

Methods to transcend mortality itself.
